title: The house I would buy if I knew I could
subtitle: A journal prompt set for naming the property you want and examining what keeps it out of reach in your mind.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 25 of the MONEY program. Use to bring the specific property goal into focus and write directly into the fears and doubts that keep it feeling impossible.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 25 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 25 of the MONEY program. The prompts go after the specific property you want to own, not the abstract concept of owning property, but the one you have half-pictured and then talked yourself out of. Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What possessions or spaces I already have make me feel grounded, secure, and proud?
Find the evidence. Even small things count.
If I knew I was allowed to own property, where would I start looking?
Name the area, the type, the rough size. Be specific.
What fears or doubts come up when I imagine owning property or significant assets? Where did those fears begin?
Write the fears. Trace them back to where you first learned them.
What is the house I would buy if I knew I could?
Describe it. Specific street if you have one. What the front door looks like. What you would do there first.
What is the one thing I believe I need to have or become before I am allowed to own it?
Name the condition your mind has set. Then ask whether it's actually true.
Adapted from Day 25 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
